name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
summary: Marjorie Taylor Greene has consistently promoted and endorsed extremist and conspiracy-driven views, including white supremacist, Islamophobic, and antisemitic ideologies. She has called for violence against Democratic politicians, compared COVID-19 safety measures to the Holocaust, and spread misinformation about the pandemic, vaccines, and election results. Greene has also advocated for the removal of LGBTQ+ rights, opposed gun control measures, and supported Russian propaganda, earning her the nickname "Moscow Marjorie." Her pattern of behavior demonstrates a disregard for facts, a willingness to spread hate speech, and a commitment to divisive and harmful ideologies.

entries.fact: Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Islamophobic, antisemitic, and white supremacist views, including the white genocide conspiracy theory.
Marjorie Taylor Greene supported calls to execute prominent Democratic Party politicians, including Hillary Clinton and Barack Obama.
Marjorie Taylor Greene equated the Democratic Party with Nazis and compared COVID-19 safety measures to the persecution of Jews during the Holocaust.
The U.S. House of Representatives voted to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from all committee roles in response to her endorsements of political violence.
Marjorie Taylor Greene filed articles of impeachment against President Joe Biden, alleging abuse of power.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Russian propaganda and praised Russian President Vladimir Putin during the Russian invasion of Ukraine.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was expelled from the conservative House Freedom Caucus after insulting fellow caucus member Congresswoman Lauren Boebert.
Marjorie Taylor Greene unsuccessfully attempted to oust Mike Johnson from his role as Speaker of the House of Representatives.

Marjorie Taylor Greene visited the U.S. Capitol and congressional offices, where she posted livestream videos outside the offices of representatives, including Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Ilhan Omar, and Rashida Tlaib, making false claims and derogatory comments.

Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that representatives Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib were not 'official' representatives because they were sworn in to Congress on the Quran, and accused them of supporting Islamic law.
Marjorie Taylor Greene began writing for the now-defunct American Truth Seekers, a conspiracy news website, which published 59 articles by Greene.
Marjorie Taylor Greene started writing for Law Enforcement Today, a pro-police fake news website, which featured 27 articles Greene authored.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was a top official of the Family America Project, a conservative group, where members posted death threats against Democrats and supported conspiracy theories.

Marjorie Taylor Greene posted a video on Facebook where she held an AR-15 style rifle and warned antifa groups to 'stay the hell out of Northwest Georgia', which was later taken down by Facebook for violating its terms of service.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's company, Taylor Commercial, received $182,300 in Paycheck Protection Program funding from the Small Business Administration during the COVID-19 pandemic, and around two months later, Greene donated $450,000 to her campaign.

Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a meme on Facebook depicting herself holding an AR-15 style rifle next to a collage of pictures of Ocasio-Cortez, Omar, and Tlaib, with the caption 'Squad's worst nightmare', which was later deleted by Facebook for violating its policies on inciting violence.

Marjorie Taylor Greene appeared at a gun rights rally in Ringgold, Georgia, where the Georgia III% Martyrs provided security, and she called Nancy Pelosi 'anti-American', a 'hypocrite', and a 'bitch' whom 'we're going to kick ... out of Congress'
Marjorie Taylor Greene's opponent, Kevin Van Ausdal, withdrew from the race, leaving Greene unopposed in the general election
Marjorie Taylor Greene made deceptive claims that her husband Perry's Floyd County voting record revealed voter fraud
Marjorie Taylor Greene wore a face mask onto the House floor that read 'Trump Won', despite Trump having lost the 2020 U.S. presidential election to Joe Biden
Marjorie Taylor Greene raised an objection to counting Michigan's electoral votes during the counting of electoral votes
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to wear a face mask while sheltering in place during the riot at the US Capitol.
Marjorie Taylor Greene tweeted that Democrats must be held accountable for the political violence inspired by their rhetoric during the debate to impeach Trump.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely suggested that the rioters at the US Capitol were members of antifa dressed as Trump supporters.
Marjorie Taylor Greene filed articles of impeachment against President Joe Biden, citing alleged abuse of power as the reason for impeachment.
Representative Jimmy Gomez announced he had drafted a resolution to expel Marjorie Taylor Greene from the House after reports that she had previously called for violence against Democrats.

Speaker Nancy Pelosi spoke of an 'enemy within the House of Representatives' and the need to increase security measures, alluding to Marjorie Taylor Greene and other Republican representatives who wanted to bring guns onto the House floor.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against the January 6 commission, a proposed commission which would have investigated the attack on the Capitol.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was one of 21 House Republicans to vote against a resolution to give the Congressional Gold Medal to police officers who defended the U.S. Capitol on January 6.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was removed from all committee assignments due to incendiary remarks she had made before her election.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promised that companies that complied with the House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack's request to retain phone records related to the attack would be 'shut down'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a conference hosted by white supremacist Nick Fuentes, at which Fuentes called the January 6 attack 'awesome' and praised Putin and Hitler.
Marjorie Taylor Greene and Congresswoman Elise Stefanik introduced a pair of resolutions to expunge both of Trump's impeachments.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was expelled from the Freedom Caucus by a vote of its members after insulting Congresswoman Lauren Boebert and going against some caucus positions.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a pair of resolutions to expunge the impeachments of Trump.
Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized Trump's support for Israeli strikes against Iran and opposed the possible involvement of the United States in the war.
Marjorie Taylor Greene announced that she will resign from her seat in the U.S. House of Representatives, effective January 5, 2026.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that the National Marrow Donor Program bill would fund aborted fetal tissue by taxpayers.
Marjorie Taylor Greene and Lauren Boebert cast two lone votes against a bill to reauthorize the National Marrow Donor Program.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that the Plan B contraceptive 'kills a baby in the womb'; Plan B actually prevents ovulation and thus prevents pregnancy, instead of terminating a pregnancy.

Marjorie Taylor Greene wrote on Twitter that 'children should not wear masks', calling recommendations by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and other public health officials 'unhealthy for their psychological, emotional, and educational growth' and 'emasculating' for boys.
Marjorie Taylor Greene opposed any form of mandatory mask-wearing, compulsory vaccination, or lockdowns in response to the pandemic.
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to get a COVID-19 vaccine, stating there was no reason to because she is 'perfectly healthy'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that vaccine passports might be 'Biden's mark of the beast', a reference to the Book of Revelation, echoing a far-right conspiracy theory that getting vaccinated is equivalent to pledging allegiance to the devil.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was fined $500 for not wearing a mask on the House floor.

Marjorie Taylor Greene called Pelosi 'mentally ill' and said that Pelosi's requirement that House members continue wearing masks until they all prove they have been vaccinated 'is exactly the [same] type of abuse' as Jews being 'put in trains and taken to gas chambers in Nazi Germany' during the Holocaust.
Marjorie Taylor Greene publicly apologized for her comments comparing vaccine rules to the Holocaust after a private visit to the Holocaust Museum in Washington, D.C.
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Biden's suggestion to provide door-to-door vaccinations to 'medical brownshirts' and said: 'You can't force people to be part of the human experiment.'
Marjorie Taylor Greene had been fined a total of $48,000 for not wearing a mask on the House floor 20 times.

Marjorie Taylor Greene sent Biden a letter calling for an investigation into Fauci over his statements on the origin of SARS-CoV-2, the virus that causes COVID-19, and called COVID-19 a 'manufactured plague' caused by a 'Chinese-made virus'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene dismissed the COVID-19 variants, including the Delta variant, saying, 'no one cares'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene posted misinformation about the virus, saying it 'is not dangerous for non-obese people and those under 65'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely said that hospitals were not overwhelmed with afflicted patients, and added, 'we're human, we can't live forever, we're going to catch all kinds of diseases and illnesses and other viruses.'
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted the anti-vaccine film Died Suddenly, which promotes misinformation about COVID-19 vaccines and Great Reset conspiracy theories.
Marjorie Taylor Greene repeatedly refused to address Fauci as Doctor, questioned his medical licence and accused him of covering up COVID-19's origins during a congressional hearing.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against a measure condemning the Myanmar coup d'état
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted to repeal the Authorization for Use of Military Force Against Iraq Resolution of 2002
Marjorie Taylor Greene stated she would kick out every Chinese person in the United States who is loyal to the Chinese Communist Party and impose strict tariffs on China
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against the bipartisan ALLIES Act, which would increase the number of special immigrant visas for Afghan allies of the U.S. military
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted in favor of H.Con.Res. 21, which directed President Biden to remove U.S. troops from Syria within 180 days
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Russian propaganda, including claims that Ukraine is a Nazi state, and praised Vladimir Putin
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed an amendment to a House foreign aid bill on Ukraine that would require all members of the House voting in favor of the bill to enlist in the Ukrainian military
Marjorie Taylor Greene was called 'Moscow Marjorie' by former Republican congressman Ken Buck due to her 'getting her talking points from the Kremlin'
Marjorie Taylor Greene called for the lifting of U.S. sanctions on Russia
Marjorie Taylor Greene was one of 18 Republicans to vote against admitting Sweden and Finland to NATO
Marjorie Taylor Greene sponsored a resolution to censure Democratic U.S. representative Rashida Tlaib for alleged 'antisemitic activity' and 'sympathizing' with Hamas
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed a defense appropriations amendment that would end U.S. funding of Israel's Iron Dome
Marjorie Taylor Greene became the first Republican to call the situation in Gaza a 'genocide'
Marjorie Taylor Greene broke with her party over the federal government shutdown, disagreeing over a proposed extension of Obamacare subsidies
Marjorie Taylor Greene said the government will never tell her how many guns she can own or how many bullets she is allowed to fire if someone were to attack her or her kids.
Marjorie Taylor Greene confronted Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg near the U.S. Capitol, questioning his gun control activism and calling him a 'coward' for ignoring her.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a bill to abolish the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives.

Marjorie Taylor Greene argued that Canada's proposed gun control laws would leave Canada 'weak and vulnerable to being invaded and easily taken over by another stronger country', such as 'Russia, who is very angry at America right now'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the July 4 Highland Park parade shooting was 'designed to make Republicans go along with more gun control' because the shooting occurred 'in a rich, white neighborhood'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that any Republican who supported the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act was 'a traitor to our party, a traitor to their voters and a traitor to our donors'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene co-sponsored the Old Glory Only Bill, a bill to ban U.S. embassies from flying pride flags.

Marjorie Taylor Greene tried to block the Equality Act while it was being debated on the House floor, saying that it would 'destroy God's creation' and 'violate everything we hold dear in God's creation'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed replacing the Equality Act with a bill that would exempt nonprofit organizations, allow people to sue the federal government 'if their religious rights are violated', and prevent trans women and girls from participating in women's sports.

Marjorie Taylor Greene supported a bill by Representative Mary Miller that would bar schools from allowing transgender students to use facilities such as bathrooms and locker rooms that correspond to their gender identity.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called transgender women 'men playing dress up' and 'literally having an identity crisis', saying, 'They never will be [women], and I refuse to recognize them that way'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized Ketanji Brown Jackson for not answering Senator Marsha Blackburn's question 'what is a woman?' and said, 'We came from Adam's rib. God created us with his hands. We may be the weaker sex, we are the weaker sex, but we are our partner's, our husband's wife'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that an entire pride month celebrating LGBT people 'needs to end'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced the 'Protect Children's Innocence Act', which would make giving gender-affirming care to transgender youth a felony punishable by up to 25 years in prison.
Marjorie Taylor Greene responded to California state senator Scott Wiener by saying that the Protect Children's Innocence Act would stop 'communist groomers' such as Wiener.
An updated version of Marjorie Taylor Greene's 'Protect Children's Innocence Act' passed the House by a vote of 216–211.
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Black Lives Matter activists to white nationalist participants at the August 2017 Unite the Right rally in Charlottesville, Virginia.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the verdict in the Derek Chauvin trial was a result of jury intimidation by Black Lives Matter, which she compared to the Ku Klux Klan.
Marjorie Taylor Greene denounced the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act, a civil rights and police reform bill, as an 'Anti-Police bill'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that Muslims who believe in Sharia law should not be in the U.S. government and that the Democratic Party is holding Black Americans as 'slaves'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called the Squad supporters of 'terrorism' and 'Hamas' who 'don't belong in Congress' and referred to them as the 'Jihad Squad'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene speculated that the Pizzagate conspiracy theory is real and said there are links between Hillary Clinton and pedophilia and human sacrifice.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed Hillary Clinton murdered her political enemies, including John F. Kennedy Jr.
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed doubt that the perpetrator of the 2017 Las Vegas shooting acted alone, suggesting it was an attack on the right to bear arms.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the August 2017 Charlottesville white nationalist rally was an 'inside job'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene agreed with a conspiracy theory known as Frazzledrip, which asserts that there is a video of Hillary Clinton and her assistant Huma Abedin murdering a child in a satanic ritual.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 9/11 was part of a U.S. government plot.
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed support for a conspiracy theory that a plane did not hit the Pentagon during the September 11 attacks.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2012 Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a false flag operation.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2018 Stoneman Douglas High School shooting in Parkland, Florida, was an organized false flag operation.
Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a video promoting the antisemitic white genocide conspiracy theory that Zionists are conspiring to flood Europe with migrants to replace the native white populations.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's Facebook account expressed support for the execution of leading Democrats, including Pelosi, Barack Obama, and Hillary Clinton, as well as support for the execution of FBI agents.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's Facebook account responded to a post asking about hanging leading Democrats, saying 'Stage is being set. Players are being put in place. We must be patient. This must be done perfectly or liberal judges would let them off.'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said Nancy Pelosi is guilty of treason and that treason is a crime punishable by death in a Facebook video.
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg had been replaced in public appearances by a body double.
Marjorie Taylor Greene followed and taunted Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg, accusing him of using children for his cause.
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed racist, antisemitic, and Islamophobic views in videos published before the Republican primary runoff election.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that Democratic National Committee staff member Seth Rich was murdered by the MS-13 gang on Obama's behalf.
Marjorie Taylor Greene told viewers that 'the only way you get your freedoms back is it's earned with the price of blood' in an interview with gun activist Chris Dorr.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg '#littleHitler' and denounced him as an 'idiot' who is trained 'like a dog'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was detailed to have support for and past ties with extremist militia groups, including the Three Percenters and the Oath Keepers, by The New York Times.

Marjorie Taylor Greene and other House Republicans were interested in launching an America First Caucus, which believes that 'America is a nation with a border, and a culture, strengthened by a common respect for uniquely Anglo-Saxon political traditions'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene attended the America First Political Action Conference, which was criticized by Senate minority leader Mitch McConnell for having 'white supremacists or anti-Semitism'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted the conspiracy theory that the U.S. government is planning to force Americans to eat fake meat grown in a 'peach tree dish' by Bill Gates.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the Robb Elementary School shooter had engaged in cross-dressing, echoing claims by right-wing politicians and media figures that the shooter was transgender.

Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed without evidence that the Robb Elementary School shooter was in the same Discord server as the teen accused of carrying out the 2022 Buffalo shooting and that there was a third party who 'groomed' both young men.

Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized the presence of the Georgia Guidestones, a megalithic granite monument, and said its ecumenical texts represented a nefarious future of 'population control' as envisioned by the 'hard left'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'People die in the cold. This Earth warming, and carbon, is actually healthy for us. It helps us to feed people, it keeps people alive. ... We need to hold Democrats accountable and defund all of their climate garbage' in an interview.

Marjorie Taylor Greene said that Democrats had designed the killing of Ayman al-Zawahiri to boost Biden in the midterms because the Russian invasion of Ukraine had not achieved this, shortly before doubting he had been killed.

Marjorie Taylor Greene posted online 'a map of hurricane affected areas with an overlay of electoral map by political party shows how hurricane devastation could affect the election', then wrote 'Yes they can control the weather … It's ridiculous for anyone to lie and say it can't be done'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted the conspiracy theory that George Soros' family collaborated with the Nazis in Hungary and is 'trying to continue what was not finished'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that the Camp Fire, a deadly Californian wildfire, could have been caused by 'what looked like lasers or beams of blue light' from 'space solar generators' in a scheme involving California governor Jerry Brown, companies PG&E, Rothschild & Co, and Solaren.
Marjorie Taylor Greene released a video statement from the White House on Jan. 6 electoral objection, objecting to the certification of the 2020 presidential election results.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'our elections should be decertified', promoting election fraud conspiracy theories.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's personal Twitter account was locked for 12 hours for multiple violations of Twitter's civic integrity policy, based on her posts including false claims about voting fraud and statements blaming electoral officials in Georgia.
Before the suspension, Greene's posts included false claims about voting fraud and statements blaming electoral officials in Georgia for their failure to act on such claims.
Greene criticized Twitter after her account was unlocked, saying 'Contrary to how highly you think of yourself and your moral platitude, you are not the judge of humanity. God is.'

Marjorie Taylor Greene's behavior was condemned by House majority leader Steny Hoyer, who gave McCarthy and other Republican House leaders an ultimatum to strip Greene of her committee seats within 72 hours.
Senate Minority Leader Mitch McConnell issued a statement attacking 'loony lies and conspiracy theories' as a 'cancer for the Republican Party', which was widely seen as targeted at Greene.

McConnell's statement said that somebody who suggested that no airplane hit the Pentagon on 9/11, that school shootings were pre-staged, and that the Clintons crashed JFK Jr.'s airplane is not living in reality.
Greene tweeted that the Republican Party's only problem are 'weak Republicans who only know how to lose gracefully' in response to McConnell's statement.
Marjorie Taylor Greene faced expulsion threat due to her incendiary social media posts
Mitt Romney said that the Republican Party's 'big tent is not large enough to both accommodate conservatives and kooks' in reference to Greene.
Kevin Cramer said that he would have 'a hard time supporting ... [Greene] being on the Education Committee' due to her positions on school shootings being staged.
Marco Rubio said that anyone arguing that the Stoneman Douglas High School shooting was a false flag is 'either deranged or a sadist'.
The House voted to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from her committee assignments with a vote of 230 to 199, with 11 Republicans joining all the Democrats.
Greene was barred from blocking anyone on her public Twitter or any other social media account while in office and was forced to pay $10,000 to cover legal fees for MeidasTouch.
Marjorie Taylor Greene broke Georgia law by claiming homestead exemptions on two properties, when only one may legally be claimed.

Common Cause filed an FEC complaint against Marjorie Taylor Greene for an alleged violation of McCain–Feingold campaign finance provisions, accusing her of implicit unlawful solicitation of unlimited contributions in an advertisement appearance made on behalf of a super PAC.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against rewarding the Capitol Police with the Congressional Gold Medal for defending the Capitol Building during the 2021 United States Capitol attack.
Marjorie Taylor Greene's personal Twitter account was permanently suspended for spreading COVID-19 vaccine misinformation.

Greene filed a federal lawsuit to block a law that allowed a candidate to be removed from a ballot after review by an administrative law judge and the Georgia Secretary of State, after several Georgia electors sought to remove her from the 2022 Republican primary ballot as unqualified for office.
A federal judge denied Greene's challenge to the law, requiring her to give evidence four days later.
Greene testified for three hours in response to the allegations that she had been involved in assisting the 2021 United States Capitol attack.

Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized the United States Conference of Catholic Bishops, accusing them of 'destroying' America with their work regarding immigrants and refugees and claiming that Satan was 'controlling the church'.
Twitter labeled two tweets by Greene as violating their rules on hateful conduct for misgendering and deadnaming Assistant Secretary for Health Rachel Levine, but did not delete them.
Perry Greene, Marjorie Taylor Greene's husband, announced that he was filing for divorce, claiming the marriage was 'irretrievably broken'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene's divorce from Perry Greene was finalized.
Greene targeted and misgendered congresswoman Sarah McBride in a series of tweets, labeling her a 'groomer'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called Nancy Pelosi a 'b****' in an angry acceptance speech after winning the Georgia GOP primary.
Marjorie Taylor Greene posted a picture of herself holding a gun next to a picture of 'The Squad', a group of Democratic congresswomen, with a caption that implied she was targeting them.
Facebook removed Marjorie Taylor Greene's post for inciting violence.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was one of the 147 Republicans who voted to overturn election results.
The US House of Representatives introduced a resolution to condemn and censure Representative Marjorie Taylor Greene of Georgia.
The House voted to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from committee assignments due to her promotion of conspiracy theories and violent rhetoric.
The US House voted to punish Marjorie Taylor Greene for her past comments and actions.
Marjorie Taylor Greene apologized for using the word 'retard' to refer to individuals with Down syndrome, saying it was a slang word and she was sorry if it was offensive to anyone.
A Democrat introduced a resolution to expel GOP Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene from the House of Representatives.
Marjorie Taylor Greene referred to COVID-19 vaccine passports as 'Biden's mark of the beast'
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed bills to fire Dr. Fauci and ban vaccine passports
Rep. Greene aggressively confronted Rep. Ocasio-Cortez, causing the New York congresswoman to raise security concerns.
Marjorie Taylor Greene defended Capitol rioters in a House floor speech.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was fined $500 for repeatedly going maskless on the House floor
Marjorie Taylor Greene called Nancy Pelosi 'mentally ill'
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared House mask mandates to the Holocaust
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared vaccine rules to the Holocaust, prompting widespread criticism.
Marjorie Taylor Greene argued that Covid escaped from a Wuhan lab and stated 'I don't believe in evolution, I believe in God'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said she doesn't 'believe in evolution'
Marjorie Taylor Greene apologized for comparing vaccine rules to the Holocaust.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was one of 21 Republicans who voted no on a bill to award the Congressional Gold Medal to police officers who responded to the January 6 attack on the US Capitol.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'no one cares' about the Delta variant
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Biden's vaccination push to Nazis
Marjorie Taylor Greene laughed off a question about children dying of Covid
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'we need to be the party of nationalism and I'm a Christian, and I say it proudly, we should be Christian nationalists'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested Southerners could 'welcome' Biden's 'police state friends' with guns
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed hospitals aren't reaching capacity due to Covid surge and said 'we can't live forever'
Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a conference founded by white nationalist Nick Fuentes.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was assigned to committees by the House Republican leadership.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was booted from the House Freedom Caucus.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against a measure condemning the military coup in Myanmar
Marjorie Taylor Greene has ties to extremist groups that are under scrutiny.
Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a rally in Catoosa County.
Marjorie Taylor Greene confronted Parkland shooting survivor with baseless claims.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a bill to abolish the ATF and auction off confiscated firearms.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said Canada's gun laws could spark a Russian invasion.
Marjorie Taylor Greene blamed a July Fourth mass shooting on an LGBTQ plot to enact more gun control.
Marjorie Taylor Greene backed a bill that would ban Pride flags at U.S. embassies.
Marjorie Taylor Greene displayed a transphobic sign in Congress.
Marjorie Taylor Greene wants to replace the LGBTQ+ Equality Act.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced an anti-trans bathroom bill in Congress.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that teaching about gay people is 'emotional child abuse'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene gave her definition of a woman, which sparked controversy.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that straight people face extinction within 150 years due to LGBTQ+ education.
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that Pride Month 'needs to end'
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a bill to make gender-affirming care for transgender youth a felony
Marjorie Taylor Greene hurled a homophobic slur at a Bay Area representative
The House passed a bill criminalizing gender-affirming care for minors, which Marjorie Taylor Greene supported
Marjorie Taylor Greene spread conspiracies about Charlottesville and 'Pizzagate'
Marjorie Taylor Greene made a post about the death of George Floyd
Marjorie Taylor Greene questioned the jury verdict in the Derek Chauvin trial
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Black Lives Matter protests to Ku Klux Klan tactics
Marjorie Taylor Greene referred to the Squad as the 'Jihad Squad' and called for their removal from Congress
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared COVID-19 vaccine efforts to the Holocaust
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed support for executing prominent Democrats, including Barack Obama, Hillary Clinton, and Nancy Pelosi
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that Muslim congresswomen should retake their oaths on the Bible
Marjorie Taylor Greene wrote an article calling for the removal of Nancy Pelosi, stating 'Police Are Now Seeking the Removal of Nancy Pelosi'
Marjorie Taylor Greene endorsed a conspiracy theory about Democrats and satanic child murder
Marjorie Taylor Greene believed in Frazzledrip, QAnon's wildest conspiracy theory
Marjorie Taylor Greene removed social media posts amid scrutiny of past controversial comments
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted a conspiracy theory about 'Jewish space lasers' causing wildfires, prompting calls for her removal from office.
Marjorie Taylor Greene's history of dangerous conspiracy theories and comments was reported
House Democrats moved to strip Marjorie Taylor Greene of her committee assignments due to her promotion of conspiracy theories and violent rhetoric.
Mitch McConnell called Marjorie Taylor Greene's views a 'cancer' for the Republican Party, condemning her promotion of conspiracy theories.
The House of Representatives voted to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from her committee assignments due to her embrace of conspiracy theories.
Marjorie Taylor Greene's history of dangerous conspiracy theories and comments was reported
Marjorie Taylor Greene was confronted by the father of a child with Down's syndrome over her use of a disabled slur at a GOP meeting.
A FEC complaint was filed against Marjorie Taylor Greene, accusing her of violating election law.
Twitter suspended Marjorie Taylor Greene for spreading covid-19 misinformation
Marjorie Taylor Greene lashed out at US Catholic bishops, showing brutal anti-Catholicism.
Marjorie Taylor Greene warned of meat grown in a 'peach tree dish' while peddling Bill Gates conspiracy
Marjorie Taylor Greene pushed conspiracy theory on Al Qaeda leader's death, asking 'Is there proof?'
Marjorie Taylor Greene was sued by Equity Prime Mortgage for allegedly failing to pay a loan, resulting in a court case.
Marjorie Taylor Greene brought a lawsuit against the Secretary of State for the State of Georgia.
Marjorie Taylor Greene appeared to celebrate the death of Pope Francis, posting a message that 'evil is being defeated by the hand of God'.
